label: Average time U.S. adults spend with friends in person each day — down from about an hour two decades ago.
source: Bureau of Labor Statistics, American Time Use Survey

label: U.S. adults who get no physical activity outside of work in a typical month — no sport, no walks, no exercise.
source: CDC, Behavioral Risk Factor Surveillance System

label: U.S. adults who reported measurable loneliness even before the pandemic.
source: U.S. Surgeon General, Our Epidemic of Loneliness and Isolation

label: U.S. adults who report having no close friends at all — a share that has quietly grown over the past decade.
source: Pew Research Center, What Does Friendship Look Like in America

label: Share of Americans who socialize in person on any given day — down from 38% a decade ago.
source: Bureau of Labor Statistics, American Time Use Survey

label: Less time per day that 15-25 year olds spend socializing in person compared to the same age group in 2003 — the steepest drop of any age group.
source: Bureau of Labor Statistics, American Time Use Survey
